evals.name: Basic scalogram visualization
evals.description: Load a signal and show a scalogram. Tests whether the agent correctly uses cwt without output arguments to get an auto-plot.
evals.prompt: Load the kobe.mat signal and show me a scalogram. The sampling frequency is 1 Hz.

context.setup: kobe.mat is on the MATLAB path. Use: load kobe

assertions.description: Calls cwt without capturing outputs to trigger auto-plot
assertions.expected: cwt(kobe, ...) or cwt(kobe, "Morse", 1) with no [cfs, f] = on the left side

assertions.description: Does not create a blank figure by capturing outputs after calling figure
assertions.expected: The code does NOT call figure() and then [cfs,f] = cwt(...) with captured outputs (which would produce a blank figure). Either uses cwt with no outputs (auto-plots), or does not call figure before a captured-output cwt. FAIL if: figure is opened and then cwt outputs are captured on the next line.

assertions.description: Specifies sampling frequency
assertions.expected: Passes 1 or SamplingFrequency=1 to cwt

evals.name: CWT coefficients AND visualization
evals.description: User needs both the numerical CWT outputs and a plot. Tests the manual scalogram reproduction pattern.
evals.prompt: I need the CWT wavelet coefficients for the kobe.mat signal (Fs=1 Hz) stored in variables, but I also want to see a scalogram plot. Give me both.

context.setup: kobe.mat is on the MATLAB path. Use: load kobe

assertions.description: Captures outputs from cwt or wt
assertions.expected: Shows [cfs, f] = cwt(...) or [cfs, f] = wt(...) or similar variable assignment capturing CWT coefficients

assertions.description: Uses imagesc for the scalogram plot
assertions.expected: imagesc(ax or imagesc(t in the plotting code

assertions.description: Plots abs(cfs), not abs(cfs).^2 or log
assertions.expected: abs(cfs) as the data argument to imagesc

assertions.description: Sets YScale to log
assertions.expected: YScale

assertions.description: Sets YDir to normal
assertions.expected: YDir

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error

evals.name: Efficient repeated CWT analysis
evals.description: User needs to compute CWT on many signals with the same parameters. Tests filter bank reuse pattern.
evals.prompt: I have 500 signals, each 2048 samples at 1000 Hz. I need to compute the CWT on all of them with the same Morse wavelet settings. What is the most efficient way to do this?

assertions.description: Creates cwtfilterbank before the loop
assertions.expected: cwtfilterbank

assertions.description: Uses wt method or FilterBank name-value pair for repeated computation
assertions.expected: wt(fb, ...) or cwt(..., 'FilterBank', fb) inside loop

assertions.description: Does not call bare cwt() inside the loop without FilterBank
assertions.expected: The loop body does NOT contain a bare cwt(signal, Fs) or cwt(signal, 'Morse', Fs) call without a FilterBank argument. PASS if: the loop uses wt(fb, ...) or cwt(..., 'FilterBank', fb). FAIL if: cwt() is called inside the loop without FilterBank, forcing filter bank reconstruction each iteration.

assertions.description: Explains why: filter bank construction is expensive
assertions.expected: Mentions that constructing the filter bank is expensive, or that rebuilding it each iteration wastes time

evals.name: Performance optimization for large signal
evals.description: User has a large slow-running CWT. Tests knowledge of performance levers.
evals.prompt: My signal is 800,000 samples at 10 kHz. The CWT is taking too long. I know my signal's energy is mostly between 200-2000 Hz. The signal endpoints are very similar in value. How can I speed this up?

assertions.description: Recommends setting FrequencyLimits to narrow the range
assertions.expected: FrequencyLimits

assertions.description: Recommends periodic boundary for performance
assertions.expected: periodic

assertions.description: Mentions the ~2x FFT cost difference for boundary
assertions.expected: References doubling of FFT length, 2N vs N, or similar performance explanation

evals.name: Complex-valued signal scalogram
evals.description: User has a complex signal and wants a combined scalogram from -Fs/2 to Fs/2.
evals.prompt: I have a complex-valued signal (trajectory data, 1117 samples, sampled every 4 hours). I want a single scalogram plot showing both positive and negative frequencies from -Fs/2 to Fs/2. How do I do this?

context.setup: npg2006.mat is on the MATLAB path. Use: load npg2006; ts = npg2006.cx

assertions.description: Recognizes cfs will be 3D for complex signals
assertions.expected: Mentions that the CWT output is 3D (scales x time x 2), has two pages, or separates analytic and anti-analytic components

assertions.description: Uses surf or plotAntiAnalyticScalogram, not imagesc with log scale
assertions.expected: surf or plotAntiAnalyticScalogram for the combined plot

assertions.description: Does not attempt imagesc with YScale=log for negative frequencies
assertions.expected: The code does NOT use imagesc with YScale='log' when the frequency axis includes negative values. PASS if: uses surf for the combined plot, or uses imagesc only for positive frequencies. FAIL if: imagesc is used with a log Y-axis and negative frequency values (log of negative is undefined).

assertions.description: Converts duration to numeric sampling frequency
assertions.expected: Converts 4 hours to numeric Fs (e.g., 1/hours(dt) or similar)

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error

evals.name: Boundary selection with reasoning
evals.description: User asks what boundary to use. Tests diagnostic reasoning about signal characteristics.
evals.prompt: I have a signal that starts with a 100 Hz oscillation and ends with a 400 Hz oscillation. The signal is 50,000 samples. What boundary setting should I use for cwt?

assertions.description: Does not recommend periodic boundary as the primary choice
assertions.expected: The agent does NOT recommend 'periodic' as the primary boundary choice. PASS if: recommends zeropad or reflection, or mentions periodic only to explain why it is unsuitable here. FAIL if: the primary recommendation is to use Boundary='periodic' for this signal.

assertions.description: Explains why periodic is bad here (endpoints differ)
assertions.expected: Explains that periodic boundary is inappropriate because the signal endpoints don't match (different frequencies at start vs end), causing wrap-around artifacts

assertions.description: Recommends zeropad or warns about reflection with oscillations at boundary
assertions.expected: Suggests zeropad, or warns that reflection can split oscillations at boundaries

assertions.description: Explains the reasoning, not just the answer
assertions.expected: Explains what happens physically with the chosen boundary (e.g., reflection splits the wave)

evals.name: Transient localization
evals.description: User wants to find where transient events occur in a signal. Tests time localization guidance.
evals.prompt: I have a seismic signal and I need to precisely identify the timing of the main shock event. How should I configure the CWT to get the best time localization?

assertions.description: Recommends lower TimeBandwidth for time localization
assertions.expected: TimeBandwidth

assertions.description: Suggests examining finest-scale coefficients
assertions.expected: Extract first row or highest-frequency scale of cfs for best time resolution

assertions.description: Mentions amor as an alternative for time localization
assertions.expected: Mentions amor wavelet or analytic Morlet as having good time localization

evals.name: CWT limitation recognition
evals.description: User has closely-spaced high-frequency tones. Tests whether agent recognizes CWT is the wrong tool.
evals.prompt: I have a signal sampled at 1000 Hz containing two tones at 490 Hz and 495 Hz. I need to clearly separate them in a time-frequency analysis. Can I use the CWT for this?

assertions.description: Identifies that CWT will struggle with this
assertions.expected: States that CWT has poor frequency resolution at high frequencies due to constant-Q property, or that the bandwidth is too wide to separate 490 and 495 Hz

assertions.description: Recommends spectrogram or STFT as alternative
assertions.expected: spectrogram

assertions.description: Optionally mentions modwpt as another alternative
assertions.expected: Mentions modwpt or wavelet packet transform as having uniform frequency bands

assertions.description: Explains why: constant-Q means broad bandwidth at high frequencies
assertions.expected: Explains constant-Q property or bandwidth proportional to center frequency

evals.name: Scalogram value interpretation
evals.description: User asks what the colorbar values mean. Tests L1 normalization understanding.
evals.prompt: I computed a CWT scalogram and the colorbar shows values around 3.5 at a particular time-frequency location. What does that 3.5 actually mean physically?

assertions.description: Explains values represent amplitude
assertions.expected: amplitude

assertions.description: Explains L1 normalization
assertions.expected: L1

assertions.description: States that abs(cfs) approximately equals signal amplitude at that frequency
assertions.expected: A unit-amplitude sinusoid gives abs(cfs) approximately 1, so 3.5 means approximately 3.5 amplitude

assertions.description: Does not say values represent energy or power
assertions.expected: The agent does NOT state that scalogram values represent energy, power, or power spectral density. PASS if: values are described as amplitude (due to L1 normalization). FAIL if: the agent says the values are energy, power, PSD, or energy density.

evals.name: Scale-frequency conversion
evals.description: User asks how scale relates to frequency for a specific wavelet configuration. Tests whether agent can explain the relationship and compute correctly.
evals.prompt: I'm using a Morse wavelet with TimeBandwidth=40 and gamma=3. My signal is sampled at 500 Hz. What frequency does scale 25 correspond to? And how would the answer differ if I used the amor wavelet instead?

assertions.description: Explains the s = f_psi / f relationship
assertions.expected: States that scale and frequency are inversely related via the mother wavelet's peak frequency

assertions.description: Correctly computes f_psi for Morse(3, 40)
assertions.expected: beta = 40/3, f_psi = (beta/gamma)^(1/gamma) / (2*pi) ≈ 0.2618 cycles/sample

assertions.description: Correctly computes the frequency in Hz for scale 25
assertions.expected: f = f_psi * Fs / s = 0.2618 * 500 / 25 ≈ 5.24 Hz (or equivalent calculation)

assertions.description: Correctly states amor peak frequency and computes the different answer
